key_insights.insight: A strength is not just what you are good at, but what strengthens you; it is characterized by love, joy, and invigoration.
key_insights.quote: A strength is any activity that strengthens you. Before you do it, you lean into it...While you're doing it, time whips by...when you're done with it you're like i i don't know i feel completed or i feel like me or i feel authentic...

key_insights.insight: The most reliable predictor of employee satisfaction is having the opportunity to use one's strengths every day and feeling a sense of person-work fit.
key_insights.quote: All sorts of really good predictive real-world outcomes are more likely to happen when someone says, firstly, I have a chance to use my strengths every day,or I love what I do and I'm good at it.

key_insights.insight: The day-to-day 'what' of a job (the actual activities) is more important than the 'who' (the people you work with) and the 'why' (the purpose of the work) in determining job satisfaction and engagement.
key_insights.quote: The what trumps the who and the why in the end like what are you actually filling your days with...

key_insights.insight: Top managers focus on individual strengths by conducting weekly check-ins, showing attention, and adjusting the job role to accommodate those strengths.
key_insights.quote: The best managers are checking in with each of their people, really light touch, like 10 minutes, 15 minutes,but every week, one-on-one... the best managers are frequently going, how was last week? How was next week?

key_insights.insight: Giving attention by sharing your reaction (not providing feedback) is critical for team members.
key_insights.quote: People don't want feedback. People want attention, which could be your reaction.

key_insights.insight: To understand the strengths within a team, managers should create conditions to enhance the team's best selves rather than assuming learning is just about information transfer and dumping it into a blank slate.
key_insights.quote: I think what I really need to dois actually just create the conditionsin which a person can express the best of themselves rather than me assuming that learning for that personis just information transferand dumping it into their blank slate.

key_insights.insight: The best managers starts with the assumption that people want to do good work.
key_insights.quote: You start by assuming this is a real human. You start by assuming this person's not trying to get one over on you,which is kind of an interesting mindset.

key_insights.insight: Instead of assuming someone's strengths can be fixed the best thing to do is find work for them where they can express their own talent.
key_insights.quote: Your job as a manager is not to make someone...Your job is to go, who the heck are you? And then can I find work or indeed awork context in which you can express you?

actionable_advice.advice: Track loved and loathed activities by dividing a page into two columns and recording activities for a week.
actionable_advice.how_to: Carry a notepad and for one week record any activity at work that strengthens you in the 'loved it' column and any activity that weakens you in the 'loathed it' column.

actionable_advice.advice: Write a love note to yourself by completing the sentence "I love it when I [verb]".
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify a specific action that you find enjoyable and strengthening, and articulate it in a simple sentence starting with "I love it when I..."

actionable_advice.advice: Schedule weekly 10-15 minute check-ins with each team member, asking what they loved/loathed last week, priorities this week, and how you can help.
actionable_advice.how_to: Set a recurring appointment for each team member, focus on those three questions.

memorable_stories: Marcus overcoming a severe stammer by pretending to speak to large groups even in one-on-one conversations.
The story of a cave painting depicting humans with animal traits collaborating to hunt, illustrating the origins of teamwork.
A head of marketing turning down a promotion because it removed him from doing the work he loved.
Housekeepers who sit in the bath and lie on the bed in order to see their work from the eyes of the future guest.
contrarian_claims: Giving constant feedback to employees is actually detrimental; attention is more important.
The notion of a singular company culture is misleading; companies consist of many different team cultures.
Repeated past behavior is the best predictor of repeated future behavior. (as opposed to the general idea that 'past behaviour' is the best predictor of future behaviour')
Competence can be a devilish curse.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - How To Find Ultimate Fulfilment At Work: Marcus Buckingham
